reasoning: New Jersey is a structurally Democratic state. In a 2025 open gubernatorial election, the Democratic nominee has around a 75% chance of winning the general election based on recent history (Democrats won the open seat in 1989, 2001, 2005, and 2017). The Democratic primary is expected to be a competitive multi-way race featuring highly-funded candidates like Mikie Sherrill, Josh Gottheimer, Steven Fulop, Steve Sweeney, and Ras Baraka. Mikie Sherrill is widely considered a top-tier candidate with strong fundraising ability and broad appeal across the state's Democratic base. Without the "county line" system, the primary will rely heavily on name recognition, fundraising, and natural base support, which likely favors Sherrill and Gottheimer. We estimate her primary win chance at roughly 35-40%. Multiplying her ~38% chance of securing the nomination by an ~75% chance of the Democratic nominee winning the general gives a net probability of roughly 28%.
